body: PT Logindo Samudramakmur Tbk (LOGD) will issue 1.75 billion new shares worth IDR 25 each, equivalent to 30.17% of its total issued and paid-up capital, to three new investors through a debt-to-equity swap scheme, reducing its outstanding debt by USD 75.23 million. The company's authorized capital will increase to USD 43.55 million, net working capital will rise to USD 16.97 million, and its debt-to-equity ratio will improve to 0.53 from 0.23.
title: PT Logindo Samudramakmur to issue 1.75 billion new shares to three new investors through debt-to-equity swap scheme.

body: PT Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper Tbk (INKP) plans to issue shelf-registration bonds worth IDR 3.5 trillion and sukuk mudharabah worth IDR 2 trillion, with coupons ranging from 7.25% to 10.75% per annum and tenors from 370 days to 5 years. The proceeds will be used to pay off loans (40%) and boost working capital, with the bonds and sukuk expected to be listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on December 4, 2024.
title: Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper to Issue Bonds and Sukuk Worth IDR 5.5 Trillion for Debt Repayment and Working Capital.

body: PT Surya Toto Indonesia Tbk (TOTO) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 123.84 billion, with each share granting an interim dividend of IDR 12, to be paid on December 12, 2024, to shareholders listed as of November 25, 2024. As of September 30, 2024, TOTO reported a net profit of IDR 188.35 billion, with unrestricted retained earnings of IDR 2.18 trillion and total equity of IDR 2.46 trillion, with its shares closing at IDR 232 per share, up 0.87% from the previous day.
title: PT Surya Toto Indonesia to Distribute IDR 123.84 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders.

body: Foreign investors purchased 4.53 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume surplus of 633.44 million shares, with top buys including PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 874.42 million shares and PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 194.75 million shares. Meanwhile, top sells included PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with 340.56 million shares, PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) with 94.96 million shares, and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) with 382.22 million shares.
title: Foreign investors purchase 4.53 billion shares in Jakarta, with net surplus of 633.44 million shares.

body: PT Modernland Realty Tbk (MDLN) saw a significant shift in ownership, with PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk's stake decreasing from 18.48% to 15.29% and PT Honoris Corporindo Pratama's stake increasing from 10.17% to 13.36%, while individual investor Haiyanto raised his stake to 9.72%. Meanwhile, other companies, including PT Golden Flower Global Resources, PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala, and PT Nippon Indosari Corpindo Tbk, made notable transactions, including share buybacks and sales, affecting their respective stakes in various companies.
title: Multiple investors and companies make significant changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT MD Entertainment TBK (MDEN) has rebranded PT Net Visi Media TBK and PT MDTV Media Technologies TBK after acquiring a controlling stake in NetV, with shareholders approving the name change with 79.12% of valid votes. The company has also undergone a board transformation, with changes to its board of commissioners and directors, including the appointment of Lie Halim as President Director of NetV.
title: PT MD Entertainment rebrands subsidiaries after acquiring controlling stake in NetV.

body: PT Lippo Cikarang Tbk (LPCK) plans to raise capital through a rights issue of up to 3 billion shares worth IDR 500 per share, which is expected to positively impact the company's financial condition and support business expansion. The corporate action, pending approval, will increase the company's authorized capital to 7 billion shares and issued paid-up capital to 5.67 billion shares, with shareholders PT Kemuning Setiatama holding 80.83% and the public holding 19.17%.
title: PT Lippo Cikarang to Raise Capital through Rights Issue of Up to 3 Billion Shares

body: PT MNC Energy Investments TBK (IATA) plans to raise capital through a rights issue, issuing 20.19 billion new shares with a face value of IDR 50 each, with proceeds to be used for working capital and investments in subsidiaries. The rights issue may potentially dilute up to 44.44% of Series B shares if investors choose not to participate, with public investors currently holding 24.38% and PT MNC Asia Holding TBK holding 47.25% of Series B shares.
title: MNC Energy Investments to Raise Capital through Rights Issue of 20.19 Billion New Shares.

body: PT Waskita Toll Road, a subsidiary of PT Waskita Karya Tbk (WSKT), has increased its stake in PT Kresna Kusuma Dyandra Marga (KKDM) through a debt-to-equity swap, with a total value of IDR 2.72 trillion, raising KKDM's issued and paid-up capital to IDR 6.18 trillion. As a result, WTR's portion in KKDM will increase to 83.37% from 76%, with the remaining portions belonging to RDPT and PT Jasa Marga Tbk (JSMR).
title: Waskita Toll Road increases stake in Kresna Kusuma Dyandra Marga through debt equity swap and capital increase.

body: PT Wintermar Offshore Tbk (WINS) has provided a corporate guarantee of USD 3.3 million to its subsidiary, PT Winpan Offshore, to secure a facility of USD 6.48 million from PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BCA). This is the second guarantee provided by WINS in recent months, following a USD 10 million guarantee for another subsidiary, PT Wintermar, in August.
title: Wintermar Offshore provides USD 3.3 million corporate guarantee for subsidiary Winpan Offshore's USD 6.48 million facility from Bank Central Asia.
